treehammock Test Suite Inventory

Generated from raptor-balcony-pr-integration-12-system-stack-expansion-unit-contract-fix on 2026-05-22.

This document inventories the unit, in-process integration, SQL integration contract, and Docker/HAProxy system integration tests in the current codebase. It is intended to help future PRs understand which test suite protects which contract before adding, moving, or deleting coverage.

Test lane overview

LaneProject / filterPurposeTypical commandExternal dependencies
Unit teststreehammock.Tests, mostly treehammock.Tests.UnitFast behavioral, contract, API-shape, SQL-text, documentation, and helper tests. These are the inner tripwires.dotnet testNone beyond .NET test runtime.
In-process integration teststreehammock.Tests.IntegrationService/controller/harness-level flows without Docker. These prove multi-step auth/reset ceremonies and app boot contracts.dotnet test .\treehammock.Tests\treehammock.Tests.csproj --configuration Debug --filter "FullyQualifiedName~treehammock.Tests.Integration"None beyond .NET test runtime.
SQL integration contractstreehammock.Tests.SqlContractsReal PostgreSQL function/repository behavior, including reset persistence and concurrency contracts.dotnet test .\treehammock.Tests.SqlContracts\treehammock.Tests.SqlContracts.csproj --configuration DebugPostgreSQL plus TREEHAMMOCK_RUN_DEFERRED_SQL_CONTRACTS=true and a connection string.
Docker/HAProxy system integrationtreehammock.Tests.SystemOutside-in tests through HAProxy with Postgres and Dragonfly containers. This is the closest local proof to runtime wiring..\eng\docker-host-system-stack-tests.cmdDocker, Postgres image, Dragonfly image, HAProxy image.

Inventory totals

Suite typeTest classesParsed test methods
Unit114977
In-process integration599
SQL integration contract521
Docker/HAProxy system integration224
Total1261121

Note: xUnit Theory methods can expand into multiple executed test cases at runtime, so parsed method counts are lower than the total test count shown by dotnet test.

Maintenance notes

  • Prefer adding fast unit or in-process integration coverage before expanding the Docker system stack. The system lane is valuable but intentionally small so it stays a practical smoke test rather than a molasses parade.
  • For SQL-backed behavior, pair text/schema contract tests with real PostgreSQL integration tests when the behavior depends on functions, locks, or persistence across service rehydration.
